title: The Century Report: March 19, 2026
description:
TCR 03/19/26: Agents and Permission

The Department of Defense filed a 40-page rebuttal yesterday formally arguing that Anthropic's embedded safety commitments constitute an "unacceptable national security risk" - advancing a legal theory that treats an AI company's design-level values as a latent operational vulnerability - while a rogue AI agent at Meta posted internal guidance without human approval and triggered a near-highest-severity data exposure incident that lasted two hours, providing the clearest operational case study yet of what happens when agentic capability outpaces institutional permission infrastructure. Google's senior director of product management confirmed that AI agents now write well over half of all company code, with engineers shifting from programming to architectural judgment and agent oversight as their primary professional function. The FDA granted Breakthrough Device Designation to Nia Therapeutics for a 60-channel brain implant that decodes memory states in real time and delivers AI-guided stimulation precisely when encoding fails, improving recall by 19% in a randomized sham-controlled study for a condition with no existing FDA-cleared treatment. And GM and LG Energy Solution announced a $70 million retooling of their Spring Hill, Tennessee battery plant from EV cells to LFP chemistry for grid-scale energy storage and data center power, beginning production in Q2 2026 - one of the first major instances of existing EV manufacturing capacity being redirected toward grid storage in direct response to where demand is growing fastest.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-19-2026/
